content.full_text:
June 25, 1976


Mark Dubois
Friends of the River
1511 "S" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Dubois:

Thank you for your letter of June 1, 1976 in which you asked
if your activities would require you to register and file lobbyist
reports. You presented the following information:

1. You might receive $200 - $300 salary as director of
   Friends of the River.

2. The organization is not presently concerned with
   legislation this year but may be involved next year.

3. The organization has invited a couple of legislators
   to visit the Stanislaus River so that they may see
   the river and become more aware of what rivers are about.

If you are currently a registered lobbyist you should be aware
that it is unlawful for a lobbyist to act as an agent or intermediary
in the making of a gift exceeding ten dollars to a legislator or arrange for the making of such a gift by any other person. Government
Code Section 86203. Thus you are prohibited from arranging the raft
trip if the trip will be made available to legislators at a price
that is substantially less than the cost of a regular commercial raft
trip. If you are a lobbyist and terminate your registration, you
will remain subject the prohibition against making gifts for six
months after filing the notice of termination. However if you are
registered as a lobbyist but were not legally required to register
because, for example, you did not receive compensation for lobbying
or did not engage in lobbying activities on a substantial or regular
basis, you may withdraw your lobbyist registration. If you file a
notice of withdrawal, you are no longer subject the prohibition
against making gift to legislators.

If you are not a registered lobbyist but you receive a salary
to communicate with legislative officials and you spend forty hours
for which compensation is received, of which at least ten hours is

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only very faint marks or artifacts visible at the top corners. There is no legible text content to transcribe from this document image.

direct communication influencing or attempting to influence legislative action you will be required to register and file lobbyist reports.

The activity described in 3. above would result in an attempt to influence legislative action. If you go on trips with the legislators you would be involved in direct communication with the legislators. The hours includible in the time cost would include the number of hours available and feasible to communicate with the legislators on each trip.

If I can be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,


Rich Eichman
Special Compliance Representative

RE:bw

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank/white with only a few faint marks or artifacts visible at the top and right edges. There is no legible text to transcribe.]

TYPED FROM HAND ITTEN

M. Dubois
Friends of the River
1611 S Street
Sacramento, Cal. 95814

June 1, 1976

Richard Eichman
FPPC
1100 K Street
Sacramento 95814

Dear Mr. Eichman:

I'm writing to you to get clarification of the
one last question I had of you. I will try to describe our
position first, and then ask the question.

Friends of the River is a grass-roots organization
concerned with preserving rivers. This year we worked for
the passage of two bills which might have preserved the
Stanislaus River - both bills faded away.

This is no particular legislation that we are
especially concerned with for the rest of this legislative
session. We might be doing legislation next year, but in the
mean time we will be doing education awareness of the resources
of our planet along with looking into other avenues in which
to preserve rivers.

We have invited a couple of legislators to visit the
Stanislaus. They will help share the expenses of the trip. We
are anxious that they see the river just so they will be more
award of what rivers are about.

Though most of my time during the past year has been
donated, there is a chance I might receive a salary ($200-300)
as director of this grass-roots organization. If I go on the
trips we do with legislators, do I have to file lobbyist report
forms?

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank white page with only a few small marks or artifacts visible at the top (two small curved marks and some specks on the right side). There is no legible text content to transcribe.

Page Two

I thank you for clearing up all my other questions

and am anxious to hear from you on this one.

Sincerely,

Mark

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with some very faint marks or artifacts visible at the top and scattered throughout, but no clearly legible text content to transcribe.
